Operation 'queryApi' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is too generic and doesn't convey the multi-purpose nature of this unified endpoint. Description lacks clarity about: (1) what each query type returns, (2) how to use the 'q' parameter as the primary selector, (3) the exclusion syntax and its purpose, (4) the response structure (ApiResponse wrapper), and (5) the mandatory User-Agent requirement. Improved version provides AI agents with clear guidance on when to use this endpoint, how to construct requests for different data types, and what to expect in responses.

data.current_summary: Query the Squiggle API for various data types
data.current_description: Single endpoint that returns different data based on the 'q' parameter. Supports teams, games, sources, tips, standings, ladder, and power queries. Prepend '!' to parameter values to exclude them (e.g., round=!1). Response format defaults to JSON.
data.summary_suggestion: Query AFL data by type and filters
data.description_suggestion: Unified endpoint for retrieving AFL data across multiple query types: teams, games, sources, tips, standings, ladder projections, and power rankings. Use the 'q' parameter to specify the data type. Filter results using optional parameters (round, team, year, etc.). Prepend '!' to any parameter value to exclude that criterion (e.g., round=!1 excludes round 1). Returns an ApiResponse object containing an array of the requested data type. Requires a descriptive User-Agent header.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic and doesn't convey the multi-purpose nature of this unified endpoint. Description lacks clarity about: (1) what each query type returns, (2) how to use the 'q' parameter as the primary selector, (3) the exclusion syntax and its purpose, (4) the response structure (ApiResponse wrapper), and (5) the mandatory User-Agent requirement. Improved version provides AI agents with clear guidance on when to use this endpoint, how to construct requests for different data types, and what to expect in responses.
